[ ा. सं.13/2/2007-ई.सी.] ार् पाल,आ् ा क सलाैका -- 22 of 44 -- II (i) 23 MINISTRY OF POWER NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 31st August, 2017 G.S.R.1125(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of section 10 read with clause (d) of sub-section (2) of section 56 of the Energy Conservation Act, 2001 (52 of 2001), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules regulating the method of recruitment for the posts of officers and other employees of the Bureau of Energy Efficiency, namely:— 1. Short title and commencement.–(1) These rules may be called the Bureau of Energy Efficiency (Recruitment, Appointment and Terms and Conditions of Service of Officers and other Employees) Rules, 2017. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Number of posts, classification and level in pay matrix.- The number of posts, their classification, level in the pay matrix attached thereto shall be as specified in columns (2) to (4) of the Schedule annexed to these rules. 3. Appointing Authority.- Subject to the overall control of the Bureau,- (a) every appointment to the post in the Bureau equivalent to Group `A’ and Group `B’ posts under the Central Government other than those specified in sub-sections (1) and (2) of section 9 of the Energy Conservation Act, 2001 (52 of 2001) shall be made by the Director-General of the Bureau; and (b) every appointment to the post in the Bureau equivalent to Group `C’ posts under the Central Government shall be made by the Secretary of the Bureau. 4. Method of recruitment, age-limit, qualifications, etc.-The method of recruitment, age-limit, qualifications and other matters relating thereto shall be as specified in columns (5) to (13) of the said Schedule. 5. Initial constitution.– (1) Every employee of the Bureau appointed before the commencement of these rules shall, on and from the date of commencement of these rules, be deemed to have been appointed under the provisions of these rules to the same post as he was holding immediately before such commencement for the purpose of seniority, promotion and other terms and conditions of the service: Provided that, the employees of the erstwhile Energy Management Centre may opt to be governed by the terms and conditions of service specified in the bye-laws of the centre, unless they opt to be governed by these rules: Provided further that, the option once excercised shall be final. (2) Any person appointed to a post in the Bureau on deputation basis, including short term contract, on completion of a period of not less than five years, may be considered, subject to his suitability having been assessed on the basis of his annual performance appraisal report by the Departmental Selection Committee, for absorption in the said post, and following such absorption, he shall be taken to have been appointed to the same post on regular basis with effect from the date he was initially appointed on deputation or short term contract basis, after having followed due process of selection, as the case may be. 6. Disqualification.- (1) No person,– (a) who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a spouse living; or (b) who, having a spouse living, has entered into or contracted a marriage with any person, shall be eligible for appointment to the said posts: Provided that, the Central Government may, if satisfied that such marriage is permissible under the personal law applicable to such person and the other party to the marriage, and there are other grounds for so doing, exempt any person from the operation of this rule. (2) Every person appointed under these rules, shall give a declaration in Form I before appointment in the Bureau. -- 23 of 44 -- 24 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] 7. Procedure for appointment.- (1) No person shall be appointed to a post in the Bureau, unless he – (a) is a citizen of India or belongs to such category of persons as may be eligible for appointment under the Central Government; (b) produces a certificate of medical fitness in Form II from a registered medical practitioner possessing a medical qualification equivalent to Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery included in one of the Schedules to the Indian Medical Council Act,1956 (102 of 1956) for appointment in the Bureau by direct recruitment; (c) submits a statement in Form III and signs the declaration, in the presence of the medical officer or medical practitioner at the time of medical examination : Provided that, no certificate of medical fitness shall be necessary if a candidate is employed or likely to be employed in a temporary capacity for a period not exceeding three months; and (d) takes an oath of allegiance in Form IV. (2) The appointing authority shall satisfy itself that the character and antecedents of a candidate selected for first appointment in the Bureau are such that they do not render such candidate unsuitable for appointment under the Central Government. 8. Saving.- Nothing in these rules shall affect reservation, relaxation of age limit and other concessions required to be provided for the Scheduled Castes, the Scheduled Tribes, the Other Backward Classes, the Ex-servicemen, and other special categories of persons in accordance with the orders issued by the Central Government from time to time in this regard. 9. Advertisement of vacancies.- (1) All appointments to the posts in the Bureau shall be made after due advertisement in the employment news and national dailies. (2) The last date for submitting applications for the posts under these rules shall be as specified in the advertisement. 10. Forms and conditions for application.–The Bureau shall specify in the advertisement the form in which applications shall be submitted, the documents and certificates and the mode of their submission and the designated officer for submission of applications. 11. Processing of application.–The respective screening committee constituted by the Director-General of the Bureau, shall consider the applications received and may shortlist candidates, based on the requirements of the job for further processing. 12. Mode of selection.-The selection committee shall recommend the candidates, on the basis of written test or interview or both, as the case may be, for finalising the selection of the shortlisted candidates. 13. Seniority.- The seniority among the employees shall be in accordance with the instructions issued by the Central Government from time to time. 14. Power to relax.–Where the Central Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient to do so, it may, by order, and for reasons to be recorded in writing and in consultation with the Bureau, relax any of the provisions of these rules with respect to any class or category of persons.
